text: Abstract: The paper deals with the development and implementation of the Czech-Polish bilingual nomenclature on railway lines in Zaolzie (the Czech part of historic Cieszyn Region) in the Czech Republic. After a short overview of history of the Polish national minority in Zaolzie, we present the activities of the Congress of Poles Abroad (CMP) dealing with issues of linguistic rights of minorities. We describe the legal and theoretical basis of bilingualism (such as the European Charter for Regional or Minority Languages) and the statutory regulations that need to be fulfilled before minority language can be introduced into road signs. The central part of the paper represents a detailed, strictly chronological presentation of the progress of bilingual nomenclature on railway stations along the main railway line connecting cities Čadca and Dolny Kubín in the Czech Republic. It shows development of proposals since 2004 up to the present state of affairs. In addition, we also observe the usage of the Polish language in the environment of the railway station, e.g. at electronic departure and arrival boards in stations or in audio station announcements read by professional lecturers. Finally, we address the administrative obstacles arising in connection with the introduction of bilingualism, as well as the incomprehension of non-uniformity of the naming in the form of different types of signs and, last but not least, some political aspects.
headline: Fully Human Written
